body_text:
21:24 â July 8, 2021
Interac
e-Transfer Limits and Limit Groups Update
Per
our communication
(June 30) all financial institutions participating in the
Interac
e-Transfer
Â®
service must support a per-transaction incoming limit of $25,000 set by
Interac
. There will be no rolling incoming limits. The incoming per-transaction limit applies to transactions that require a security question to be answered, Autodeposit enabled transfers, or in future, account-to-account transactions.
Interac
is making a global change across the Interac e-Transfer service. This means FIs must permit the deposit of single Interac e-Transfer transactions valued at up to $25,000 and canât restrict the cumulative amount deposited from multiple transactions. The increased transaction limit will apply to all e-Transfer transactions, including Send Money, Autodeposit, and Request Money. The higher limit will also apply to Interac e-Transfer for Business (Interac Instant) transactions.
To reflect these changes, we made the following updates:
All limit groups now support an incoming per transaction limit of $25,000.
All incoming rolling limits have been removed.
The probationary limit feature has been removed as
Interac
no longer offers it
.
Added Limit Group 16 with the highest Send Money limits of up to $25,000. If your financial institution wants to participate in this group, contact your Central 1 Relationship Manager to discuss customer experience, management of risk, and fraud.
Interac is updating their language usage for âInstant Transferâ feature in limit groups to âNo Delay Transferâ effective July 18, 2021. We have included that terminology update with this Release.
For existing limit groups, the current outgoing value limits for Send Money remain unchanged to minimize impact to financial institutions that are already participating in limit groups.
We renamed âInterac e-Transfer Settlement, Tracing, and Reportsâ to âInterac e-Transfer Limits, Settlement, Tracing, and Reports.â Please see the table below for further details.
Electronic Transactions Volume
Interac e-Transfer Limits, Settlement, Tracing, and Reports Part
Section 2.1, Terminology
We added a definition for âNo Delay Transferâ and updated the definition for âRolling Time Periodâ to reflect that it only applies to Send transactions.
Section 2.2, Frequently Asked Questions
New
Chapter 4, Interac e-Transfer Limits and Central 1 Limit Groups
New
Section 4.1, Interac e-Transfer Limits
This information was previously in Section 1.3. We updated the text to explain that limits only apply to Send transactions.
Section 4.2, Central 1 Limit Groups
We incorporated the Limit Group details previously published in the executive overview. Limit groups 14, 15, and 16 are new.
